svg.title: Full recommendation pipeline from Kafka event to genre output
svg.desc: Shows JSON event entering Kafka, stored in Redis, user vector computed, FAISS queried, and genres extracted from top movie results
svg.text: ① Event JSON — what enters Kafka
genres are NOT sent in the event. they are looked up from metadata at the end.
② Kafka → Spring Boot consumer → Redis sliding window
key: user_events:u_42 → stores last 50 raw event objects, newest first
also cached: user_genres:u_42 → historically liked genre scores (separate key)
③ ML service builds user vector (Python / FastAPI)

g.text: weighted average of ALS item embeddings

svg.text: V[318], V[550], V[110] are rows from item_embeddings.npy — trained once offline by ALS.
live events just use these as weights. no retraining needed.
④ FAISS returns top-200 movie indices + scores
⑤ Genre extraction ← answer to your Q8

g.text: genre_score[g] = sum of ANN scores for all movies tagged with genre g
output: ranked genre list + top-N movies per genre
Drama · Thriller · Crime → pass to ranker or return directly
